title: Streaming-mapped descriptor ring outside coherent CMA
kernel_subject: mmc: test A733 streaming descriptor ring

test_setup.bootargs: console=ttyS0,115200n8 earlycon=uart8250,mmio32,0x02500000 loglevel=8 ignore_loglevel drm_debug=1 root=PARTUUID=db375e07-7682-4d4e-b8bc-a923dd0b027e rootfstype=ext4 rootwait ro rootflags=noload init=/bin/sh

observations: A733 descriptor ring used normal zeroed memory mapped with dma_map_single(DMA_BIDIRECTIONAL), not dma_alloc_coherent.
Descriptor streaming allocation logged sg_dma=0xfa800000, pages=2, size=8192.
The data buffer also landed below 4 GiB at sg0 dma=0xfa802000.
Card init still reached SDXC detection and mmcblk0 creation before the forced CMD18 IDMA read.
Before command launch: DLBA=0x3ea00000, descriptor d0={8000001c,00001000,3ea00800,3ea00004}, stamp d1={00000000,a733016e,dead0001,beef0002}, checksum=0x01bb7325.
After post-data poll and dma_sync_single_for_cpu: descriptor words and checksum were unchanged, OWN stayed set, CHDA=DLBA=0x3ea00000, CBDA=0, IDST=0x4000, CBCR=0x400, BBCR=0.
conclusion: Changing the descriptor ring from coherent CMA allocation to streaming-mapped normal memory did not let A733 SDMMC0 IDMAC consume descriptor word 0. H019 closes coherent/CMA descriptor allocation class as the immediate cause. The current blocker is below descriptor memory class: SDMMC0 IDMAC master/fabric reachability, address translation, security/firewall, or an unobserved non-SDMMC control path.
